title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The factory released toxic _____ into the river, killing thousands of fish.
explanation: Toxic waste refers to harmful materials discarded by factories. Pollution often involves releasing waste into water or air.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Carbon _____ from cars and factories are a major cause of climate change.
explanation: Emissions are gases released into the atmosphere. Carbon emissions (CO2) from burning fossil fuels drive global warming.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Many countries are investing in _____ energy sources like wind and solar power.
explanation: Renewable energy comes from sources that naturally replenish, such as sunlight, wind, and water.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: We should _____ plastic bottles instead of throwing them in the trash.
explanation: To recycle means to process used materials so they can be made into new products. After 'should' we need the base form of the verb.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Global _____ refers to the gradual increase in the Earth's average temperature.
explanation: Global warming is the long-term rise in Earth's temperature caused mainly by greenhouse gases.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Air _____ in big cities can cause serious respiratory problems.
explanation: Pollution is the presence of harmful substances in the environment. Air pollution affects breathing and health.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The _____ effect traps heat in the Earth's atmosphere.
explanation: The greenhouse effect is a natural process where certain gases trap heat, warming the planet. Human activity has intensified it.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Deforestation means the large-scale _____ of trees from forests.
explanation: Deforestation is the clearing or removal of forests, often for agriculture or development.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Many species face _____ because their natural habitats are being destroyed.
explanation: Extinction means a species no longer exists anywhere on Earth. Habitat loss is a leading cause.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: A _____ is a long period without rain that damages crops and water supplies.
explanation: A drought is an extended period of unusually low rainfall, leading to water shortages.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Solar _____ convert sunlight directly into electricity.
explanation: Solar panels (also called photovoltaic panels) capture sunlight and convert it into electrical energy.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The country held a general _____ to choose a new president.
explanation: An election is a formal process where people vote to choose a leader or representative.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The _____ passed a new law to protect workers' rights.
explanation: The government is the group of people who officially run a country and make laws.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Every citizen has the right to _____ in a democratic election.
explanation: To vote means to formally express your choice in an election. It is a fundamental democratic right.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The new _____ makes it illegal to dump chemicals in rivers.
explanation: A law is an official rule created by the government that everyone must follow, with legal consequences for breaking it.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The prime minister gave a speech in _____ about the new education policy.
explanation: Parliament is the group of elected officials who make and change laws in many countries.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: A _____ is a system of government where the people choose their leaders by voting.
explanation: In a democracy, power belongs to the people, who exercise it through free elections.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The politician started a _____ to win support before the election.
explanation: A campaign is an organized effort to achieve a goal, especially to win an election.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The newspaper _____ read: 'Historic Peace Agreement Signed'.
explanation: A headline is the title of a newspaper article, printed in large letters at the top.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The _____ interviewed witnesses at the scene of the accident.
explanation: A journalist is a person who gathers, writes, and reports news for media outlets.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The TV station broadcast a live _____ from the disaster area.
explanation: A report is a factual account of an event, presented in news media. After 'a live' we need a singular countable noun; 'reports' (plural), 'reporting' (gerund) and 'reported' (past participle) don't fit.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: You should always check if a news _____ is reliable before sharing it.
explanation: A source is where information comes from. Checking sources helps avoid spreading false information.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The spread of false information online is called _____.
explanation: Misinformation is false or inaccurate information, especially when spread regardless of intent to deceive.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The editor decided which stories to _____ in tomorrow's newspaper.
explanation: To publish means to make written content available to the public through print or online media.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Millions of people live in _____, unable to afford basic necessities like food and shelter.
explanation: Poverty is the state of being extremely poor, lacking money for basic human needs.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The organization fights for gender _____ in the workplace.
explanation: Equality means the state of being equal, especially in rights and opportunities regardless of gender.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: _____ is the unfair treatment of people based on their race, age, or gender.
explanation: Discrimination means treating someone unfairly because of characteristics like race, gender, or age.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Many refugees flee their countries because of war and _____.
explanation: Persecution is cruel or unfair treatment, especially because of race, religion, or political beliefs.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The debate about _____ policy focuses on who should be allowed to enter the country.
explanation: Immigration policy deals with rules about people moving to a new country to live there permanently.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: A _____ is a person who has been forced to leave their country due to war or disaster.
explanation: A refugee is someone who flees their home country to escape danger, war, or persecution.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Thousands of people joined the _____ to protest against the new tax.
explanation: A demonstration (or protest march) is a public gathering where people express opposition to something.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The charity works to reduce _____ by providing homes for people living on the streets.
explanation: Homelessness is the condition of not having a permanent place to live.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The charity works to raise _____ about the impact of plastic pollution on ocean wildlife.
explanation: Awareness means knowledge or understanding of a situation. Raising awareness means helping people learn about an issue.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Freedom of _____ means people can say what they think without government punishment.
explanation: Freedom of speech is the right to express opinions publicly without censorship or government interference.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The workers went on _____ to demand better pay and conditions.
explanation: A strike is when workers refuse to work as a form of protest, usually to pressure employers for better conditions.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: A _____ is a formal request signed by many people asking an authority to take action.
explanation: A petition is a document signed by many people demanding action from a government or organization.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The company was accused of _____ by paying women less than men for the same job.
explanation: Inequality means an unfair difference in treatment or opportunity. Pay inequality between genders is a major social issue.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The government introduced a _____ to ban single-use plastic bags.
explanation: A policy is an official plan or set of rules adopted by a government or organization.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The journalist was praised for her _____ reporting on government corruption.
explanation: Investigative reporting involves deep research to uncover hidden facts, especially about wrongdoing.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Burning fossil _____ like coal and oil releases greenhouse gases.
explanation: Fossil fuels are natural substances like coal, oil, and gas formed from ancient organisms, burned for energy.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The melting of polar ice _____ is causing sea levels to rise.
explanation: Ice caps are large masses of ice covering the polar regions. Their melting contributes to rising sea levels.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The country's _____ rate measures the percentage of people without jobs.
explanation: The unemployment rate shows what share of the workforce is jobless and actively seeking work.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: A _____ footprint measures how much carbon dioxide your activities produce.
explanation: A carbon footprint is the total amount of greenhouse gases generated by a person's actions.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: An _____ is a person who campaigns energetically to bring about political or social change.
explanation: An activist works actively to promote political or social change, often through protests, campaigns, or advocacy.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The city installed more _____ bins to encourage people to sort their rubbish.
explanation: Recycling bins are containers for collecting materials like paper, glass, and plastic to be processed and reused.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The _____ signed the international agreement to reduce carbon emissions.
explanation: Delegates are people chosen to represent a group at a conference or meeting, such as international climate talks.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The news _____ reported that the storm had caused widespread damage.
explanation: A news bulletin is a short broadcast or published report giving the latest important news.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Sustainable development meets present needs without _____ future generations' ability to meet theirs.
explanation: Sustainable development balances current needs with protecting resources so future generations can also thrive.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: The protesters called for social _____, demanding fair treatment for all citizens.
explanation: Social justice is the idea that everyone deserves equal economic, political, and social rights and opportunities.

title: EN B1+ Vocab 1: Society, Environment, News
question: Media _____ means having the skills to critically evaluate news and information.
explanation: Media literacy is the ability to access, analyze, and evaluate media messages critically and create content.
